branch_types.description: branch declared inside a canonical procedure
branch driven by a declared boolean predicate over context vector
branch selected from observed context dimensions
branch entered after a declared failure class
branch entered to restore a safe state
branch that hands off to a higher tier or OEM
branch entered to resolve an ambiguous input

rules: branches are declared, not invented at runtime
branch selection is deterministic given the context vector
each branch carries provenance (which input(s) selected it)
branches preserve safeguards from the parent procedure
branch transitions never silently change user intent
branches must terminate (no infinite loops; cap by retry thresholds)
